changelog:
## v0.33.3 (2026-04-20) — 40+ new formats, HVSC MD5 correct for real, diagnostic shutdown trace

**New format support — 40+ additional file types.**

- **Console chiptunes via libgme 0.6.4** (new dependency, LGPL-2.1, statically linked). Playable: Game Boy `.gbs`, Super Nintendo `.spc`, Sega Megadrive/32X/Master System/Game Gear/Mega CD/SG-1000/SC-3000/BBC Micro/ColecoVision `.vgm` / `.vgz`, ZX Spectrum / Amstrad CPC `.ay`, NES `.nsf` / `.nsfe`, MSX `.kss`, PC Engine / TurboGrafx-16 `.hes`, Atari 8-bit `.sap`, Sega SG-1000 `.sgc`, Sega Genesis / Mega Drive `.gym`. Each format renders at 44.1 kHz stereo through the standard VST3 chain + WASAPI output. Track lengths are pulled from the file's own metadata tags (libgme falls back to a 150 s default when untagged) with an 8 s fade-out at end-of-track so chiptunes don't clip into the next playlist entry. Multi-subtune files are playable from their first track in this release — subtune navigation UI is planned for v0.34.
- **30+ additional tracker formats via the existing libopenmpt 0.8.6.** The decoder dispatch already carried most extensions; v0.33.3 adds the missing ones and wires them through the file browser, playlist, file dialog and metadata panel. New entries: Digibooster Pro `.dbm` / `.digi`, DSIK `.dtm`, General DigiMusic `.gdm`, Graoumf Tracker `.gtk` / `.gt2`, Imago Orpheus `.imf`, Liquid Tracker `.liq`, Un4seen MO3 `.mo3`, Octalyser `.okta`, Disorder Tracker 2 `.plm`, SoundFX `.sfx`, Soundtracker Pro II `.stp`, STMIK `.stx`, TCB Tracker `.tcb`, X-Tracker `.xtr`. See the format table in the User Guide for the complete list.
- **PSF / miniPSF (Playstation), SNDH (Atari ST), and the UADE Amiga-exotic family (TFMX, Fred, Hippel, SoundMon, Sonic Arranger) remain out of scope for this release.** Each requires a full CPU emulator; they're tracked in `docs/dev/UNFINISHED_FEATURES.md` for a future dedicated "exotic emulators" release.

**HVSC SID MD5 — finally correct.** The canonical-hash for SID subtunes shipped wrong in four successive releases (v0.32.5, v0.32.6, v0.33.1, v0.33.2) because each "fix" re-derived the algorithm from partial documentation instead of running an actual test vector against the user's own `Songlengths.txt`. v0.33.3 computes the hash variant-by-variant in Python against Rob Hubbard's *Commando.sid* (HVSC canonical MD5 = `7fe08f19e769b3e6ce03249cb3d00b5b`) until a variant matches, then ports the matching recipe to C++. The missing detail every earlier attempt lost: when the PSID header's load-address field is zero, the first two bytes of the data section are a C64-PRG-style embedded load address and must be skipped before hashing the body. Net effect: HVSC lookup now actually hits for the large majority of the 60 572-entry database on first add, cache restore, and session reload.

**IFF 8SVX — parser rewritten end-to-end.** Previous releases silently ignored the `ctOctave` and `sCompression` fields in the Voice Header. Files encoded with Fibonacci or Exponential delta compression produced ~1 s of noise and then the engine's EOF heuristic terminated playback — closely matching user-reported "abort after 1 second". v0.33.3 reads the full VHDR, refuses compressed bodies with a clear log line rather than feeding garbage into the pipeline, and for multi-octave samples plays back only octave 1 (native pitch) rather than concatenating progressively higher-pitched copies. The open log now also reports oneShot/repeat counts, octave count, compression type and BODY byte count so future reports land with evidence.

**Plugin cache — explicit load log.** A clean v2 cache load was previously silent at startup, so users reasonably concluded the scanner was re-probing every plugin on each launch. New `VST3Scanner: cache loaded — N plugins across M file(s) (v2 schema, from plugin_cache.json)` line fires immediately after the cache parse, making the difference between cache-hit and full rescan obvious in the log.

**Shutdown — diagnostic trace.** Clean shutdown now emits a full sequence of markers: `Shutdown: Qt event loop returned (code N)`, `Shutdown: settings persisted`, `Shutdown: audio engine stopped`, `PluginChain: shutdown complete, N plugin(s) unloaded`, `WASAPI: audio device closed`, `Shutdown: session lock released`, and the existing `=== Session ended cleanly ===`. Before v0.33.3 only the last two lines were logged; a hang between event-loop exit and lock release was invisible.

**Waveform provider — capacity leak fix (partial).** `WaveformProvider::clear()` now shrinks the live-peak vectors between tracks, releasing a few MB per URL-stream → URL-stream transition. The primary driver of the 443→762 MB 18-minute growth reported in FXC-BUG-0332-006 is still under investigation; decoder swap cleanly releases `fileData_` and cover art is on disk not in memory, so the remaining source must be elsewhere. The heartbeat line already reports `rss={N}MB` per tick, which will narrow the cause in the next incident.

**Dependency check.** Pre-release sweep of all third-party libraries. spdlog 1.17.0, nlohmann/json 3.12.0, and the newly-added libgme 0.6.4 are pinned to their latest upstream stable tags. Vendored libraries (Monkey's Audio, TagLib, SQLite, pugixml, dr_libs) have minor bugfix bumps available but no breaking changes or security-urgent fixes; those are batched for v0.34 to keep v0.33.3's validation surface small.
